title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. filix-mas
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. filix-mas, also known as the male fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris filix-mas plant, a type of evergreen fern native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including constipation and indigestion, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. filix-mas include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, or made into a tincture or decoction, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
